instruction: I just made some changes to my personal site and checked out master, but now I can't find those changes. Please help me find them and merge them into master.
finalMessage:
Recovered commit `74e4785` from the reflog and merged it into `master` as `b10eee1`.

- Resolved the conflict using the newer Stanford content.
- Working tree is clean.
- Build tools were not installed, so no site build was available.

trajectory.summary:
git status --short --branch && printf '
-- branches --
' && git branch -avv && printf '
-- reflog --
' && git reflog --date=local -20 && printf '
-- recent all --
' && git log --all --oneline --decorate --graph -20

git show --stat --oneline --decorate 74e47856b0e14101a816735bfd27bc8f39328be7 && printf '
-- commit details --
' && git show --format=fuller --no-ext-diff 74e47856b0e14101a816735bfd27bc8f39328be7 -- && printf '
-- anc
git show --stat --oneline d7d3e4b && git show --format= --no-ext-diff d7d3e4b -- _includes/about.md _layouts/default.html && git status --porcelain
git merge --no-ff 74e47856b0e14101a816735bfd27bc8f39328be7 -m "Merge recovered Stanford site changes" || true

printf '%s\n' '-- project files --'; ls -la | sed -n '1,100p'; printf '%s\n' '-- config candidates --'; find . -maxdepth 2 -type f \( -name 'Gemfile' -o -name 'package.json' -o -name 'README*' \) -print; if [ -f README.m
if command -v bundle >/dev/null 2>&1; then bundle exec jekyll build; else echo 'bundle not installed' >&2; exit 127; fi
git status --short --branch; printf '\n'; command -v bundle || true; command -v jekyll || true; git show --stat --oneline --summary HEAD
